content:
Remember this: The more you study grammar in this course, the better you'll get at identifying the sentences in your drafts where grammar needs to be corrected.

Spelling errors are yet another issue that can plague college written work. You must take your time while proofreading to avoid misspelling the names of people, places, organizations, etc.

Being aware of these common pitfalls will help you improve your overall quality of writing by avoiding these simple yet significant issues!

Let's eat Grandma! vs. Let's eat, Grandma! Punction saves lives.

After you finish this page, click Next to continue to How to Avoid Making Mistakes with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling.

Week 1 | How to Avoid Making Mistakes with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling
Grammar, Punctuation, Spelling Excellent: Tips for Error Avoidance
To avoid making grammar, punctuation, and spelling mistakes in college writing, you can do a few things. First and foremost, always proofread your work before submitting it. Take the time to read through your essay carefully and make sure everything is correct.
